title: Brilliant Brains and Big Ideas Day
story: Every February 11, we celebrate the creative minds who dream up amazing inventions that change our world! National Inventors' Day honors everyone from famous inventors like Thomas Edison to kids who build cool gadgets in their garages. People visit science museums, try hands-on invention workshops, and share photos of their own creative projects online. Schools often host invention fairs where students show off their brilliant ideas. Some families even spend the day building something new together using cardboard, tape, and imagination. It's the perfect time to think like an inventor and ask, "What problem can I solve today?"
activity_question: What do schools often host on National Inventors' Day?
follow_up_question: What everyday problem would you love to invent a solution for?

title: Making Friends Never Goes Out of Style
story: Every February 11, people across the country celebrate National Make a Friend Day by reaching out to someone new! Kids might sit with a classmate they've never talked to at lunch, join a different group at recess, or introduce themselves to a neighbor. Adults often strike up conversations at coffee shops, join community groups, or reconnect with old acquaintances on social media. Some schools organize friendship activities where students write kind notes to each other or play games that help them learn about their classmates. The holiday reminds everyone that friendship can start with just a simple "hello" and a smile.
activity_question: According to the story, how might kids celebrate National Make a Friend Day at school?
activity_choices: Sit with someone new at lunch or join a different group at recess
Bring their pet hamster to show-and-tell
Skip all their classes to play outside
Only talk to their best friends all day
activity_answer: Sit with someone new at lunch or join a different group at recess
follow_up_question: What's the most creative way you can think of to make a new friend?

title: Minty Fresh Fun on Peppermint Patty Day
story: Chocolate lovers unite every February 11 for National Peppermint Patty Day! This sweet holiday celebrates the perfect combo of cool mint and rich chocolate that makes your taste buds tingle. People mark the day by munching on their favorite peppermint patties, from classic York patties to homemade versions. Some families even host peppermint patty taste tests to find the mintiest treat. Bakeries often create special peppermint chocolate desserts, and social media fills with photos of people's minty creations. It's the one day when having chocolate for breakfast feels totally justified!
activity_question: What two flavors combine to make peppermint patties special?
bonus_fact: The York Peppermint Pattie was invented in 1940 in Pennsylvania!

title: Don't Cry Over Spilled Milk Day
story: Spilled your juice this morning and felt upset? February 11 is National Don't Cry Over Spilled Milk Day, when people practice letting go of small mistakes! This holiday reminds us that accidents happen and getting mad won't fix them. People celebrate by sharing stories about silly mishaps they've learned from, posting encouraging messages online, and teaching kids that it's okay to mess up sometimes. Some families even intentionally spill a little milk and laugh it off together, showing that minor problems aren't worth big tears.
activity_question: What do some families do together on this holiday to show that small accidents are okay?
activity_choices: Intentionally spill a little milk and laugh it off
Drink milk from fancy glasses all day
Only eat cereal without any milk
Write apology letters to their spilled drinks
activity_answer: Intentionally spill a little milk and laugh it off
follow_up_question: What's a small mistake you made that taught you something important?
